Segment_Patient_D.Segment_Patient_D.Category|1: (6) Expectation that competence needs will not be met

Segment_Summary.Segment_Summary.Main_topics_discussed|: The patient shares his difficulty in feeling emotions, the emotional distance he experiences even when he is exposed to events that are difficult and sad, which should evoke corresponding emotions in him
Segment_Summary.Segment_Summary.Self_state(s)|: The patient has an expectation that his competence needs will not be met, that he cannot feel the proper emotions, and identify with the pain of others. In response to this, he is critical of himself.
Segment_Summary.Segment_Summary.Interventions|: The therapist mainly asks exploratory questions to expand the patient's emotions and thoughts.
Segment_Summary.Segment_Summary.Relationships|: The patient-therapist relationship appears to be  positive and recpectful.The therapist is actively attentive to the patient,, and the patient responds to the expanding interventions that the therapist provides
Segment_Summary.Segment_Summary.Patient’s_micro-level_ou: the levels of insight  in this segment are well, and that his overall level of expiriencing is low is relatively good.
Segment_Summary.Segment_Summary.Context|: The patient says that he is afraid that his good friend's grandmother will die and he did not know how to feel about it

Segment_Patient_D.Segment_Patient_D.Category|2: (6) Expectation that competence needs will not be met

Segment_Summary.Segment_Summary.Main_topics_discussed|: The patient's dominant self-state includes his wish to be able to express his emotions, while giving legitimacy to the expression of it. This is talked also in the context of the patient's trying to relate to others. The self-state that includes his expectations for his inability to express and connect emotionally, is also expressed in this segment but to a lesser extent.
Segment_Summary.Segment_Summary.Self_state(s)|: The patient's dominant self-state includes his wish to be able to express his emotions, while giving legitimacy to the expression of it. This is talked also in the context of the patient's trying to relate to others. The self-state that includes his expectations for his inability to express and connect emotionally, is also expressed in this segment but to a lesser extent.
Segment_Summary.Segment_Summary.Interventions|: The therapist understands the patient's experience and provides meaning his tendency to hold back his emotions. The quality of the interventions is moderate. The therapist does not encourage the patient to express his emotions. Amplifying the patient's expression of emotions and providing legitimacy to it, could have been helpful.
Segment_Summary.Segment_Summary.Relationships|: The relationship between the patient and therapist appears positive, the dialog is fluint' the claint reacts positiv to the intervation.
Segment_Summary.Segment_Summary.Patient’s_micro-level_ou: the levels of regulation and insight of the patient in this segment are well, and that his overall level of well-being is relatively good.

Segment_Patient_D.Segment_Patient_D.Category|1: (4) Expectation that autonomy needs will not be met

Segment_Summary.Segment_Summary.Main_topics_discussed|: The patient presents situations in which he feels the need to lighten the atmosphere or find a compromise. When other people are very sad or angry he feels very uncomfortable and wants to change the atmosphere. The patient states that this desire probably comes from his father.
Segment_Summary.Segment_Summary.Self_state(s)|: The patient desires for ability to assist people, resolve conflicts, and offer support during challenging times. It appears that sometimes the other accept his help, while at other times, they blokes it. Situations where people are sad or angry make discomfort and a tendency in him to immediately lighten the atmosphere through a fight reaction.
Segment_Summary.Segment_Summary.Interventions|: The therapist asks exploratory questions to deeper into the patient's emotions and thoughts, helping to give meaning to his feelings and reactions, , reflecting his desires and connecting current experiences with past ones.
Segment_Summary.Segment_Summary.Relationships|: The patient-therapist relationship appears to be quite positive and open; the therapist is actively attentive to the patient, supports him, and the patient responds to the expanding interventions that the therapist provides
Segment_Summary.Segment_Summary.Patient’s_micro-level_ou: It seems that the levels of regulation and insight of the patient in this segment are well, and that his overall level of well-being is relatively good.
